div.h2: Claude responded: The error is straightforward: Vite can't find .

div.p: The error is straightforward: Vite can't find ./ProductDetail.css imported in src/components/ProductDetails.jsx (line 23).
The problem: There's a filename mismatch. The component file is ProductDetails.jsx (with an s) but it's trying to import ProductDetail.css (without an s).

div.p: Most likely fix: Just check what your CSS file is actually named in the src/components/ folder and update the import to match exactly (these are case-sensitive on Linux/Mac).

div.h2: Claude responded: Now it's complaining that the image files don't exist.

div.p: Now it's complaining that the image files don't exist. The JSX imports Frenzy_fa__2_.png (double underscore) but your actual filenames might differ.
Go to your src/assets/ folder and check the exact filenames. Then let me know what they're actually called and I'll fix all the imports in the JSX for you.
Or, the quickest fix: rename your asset files to match what the code expects:

div.h2: Claude responded: That's because the ProductDetails.

div.p: That's because the ProductDetails.jsx file controls the general product detail page, but looking at the URL (/products/kMWwBzGjtfIJv0PPQzDN) and the layout you're seeing, this page is actually being rendered by ProductFrenzyFA.jsx — which has its own separate imports that we haven't touched.
The images are already correctly imported in ProductFrenzyFA.jsx from the start — the issue is likely that the actual image files don't exist yet in your src/assets/ folder.
Check if these files are in src/assets/:

ul.class: [li_&]:mb-0 [li_&]:mt-1 [li_&]:gap-1 [&:not(:last-child)_ul]:pb-1 [&:not(:last-child)_ol]:pb-1 list-disc flex flex-col gap-1 pl-8 mb-3

code.class: bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]
bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]
bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]

div.p: If they're missing, you need to add them there. Do you have those image files somewhere on your computer?

div.h2: Claude responded: The files exist!

div.p: The files exist! The issue is the CSS import error shown in the browser tab — "Vite CSS import file not found". That means ProductFrenzyFA.css is missing from your src/components/ folder.
Can you scroll up in that assets folder and also check your src/components/ folder — is ProductFrenzyFA.css there?

div.h2: Claude responded: I can see clearly from the search results:

div.p: I can see clearly from the search results:
ProductPage is referenced in:

ul.class: [li_&]:mb-0 [li_&]:mt-1 [li_&]:gap-1 [&:not(:last-child)_ul]:pb-1 [&:not(:last-child)_ol]:pb-1 list-disc flex flex-col gap-1 pl-8 mb-3
ul.li: App.jsx — imports and uses it as a route

div.p: So you cannot delete ProductPage.jsx and ProductPage.css yet — you need to first remove it from App.jsx.
ProductGrid is only referenced inside ProductGrid.jsx itself and ProductPage — so it's safe to delete both:

ul.class: [li_&]:mb-0 [li_&]:mt-1 [li_&]:gap-1 [&:not(:last-child)_ul]:pb-1 [&:not(:last-child)_ol]:pb-1 list-disc flex flex-col gap-1 pl-8 mb-3

div.p: Then let me fix App.jsx to remove the ProductPage import and route, and after that you can delete those too. Want me to do that now?

div.p: Need to check first — search these in VS Code (Ctrl + Shift + F):

ProductFrenzyB — same check, delete ProductFrenzyB.jsx + ProductFrenzyB.css

div.p: From what I can see in your App.jsx, those two still have routes:

div.h2: Claude responded: The images aren't showing because Google Images links don't work as direct image URLs — they're links to web pages, not direct links to the image file itself.

div.p: The images aren't showing because Google Images links don't work as direct image URLs — they're links to web pages, not direct links to the image file itself.
To get a working image URL you need to:

ol.class: [li_&]:mb-0 [li_&]:mt-1 [li_&]:gap-1 [&:not(:last-child)_ul]:pb-1 [&:not(:last-child)_ol]:pb-1 list-decimal flex flex-col gap-1 pl-8 mb-3
ol.li: Go to Google Images and find your image
Click on the image to open it
Right-click the image → "Open image in new tab"
Copy the URL from that new tab — it should end in .jpg, .png, or .webp

div.p: Or even better, use a free image hosting site:

ul.class: [li_&]:mb-0 [li_&]:mt-1 [li_&]:gap-1 [&:not(:last-child)_ul]:pb-1 [&:not(:last-child)_ol]:pb-1 list-disc flex flex-col gap-1 pl-8 mb-3
ul.li: Imgur (imgur.com) — upload your image, right-click the image on the page → copy image address
ImgBB (imgbb.com) — upload and get a direct link

div.p: The URL must end in an image extension like .jpg or .png to work in your admin panel.
